channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Meta AI app climbs to No. 5 on the App Store after Muse Spark launch
item.description: The app was ranking No. 57 on the App Store just before Meta AI's new model launched. Now, it's No. 5 — and rising.

item.title: Netflix adds three Jackbox games to its TV app
item.description: Netflix has added a heavy hitter in party activities to its gaming lineup. The streamer announced that Jackbox Party Essentials is joining the Netflix Party Games collection. This move makes three of the popular Jackbox group games available for free to Netflix subscribers: Fibbage 4, Quiplash 3 and Drawful 2. Each one supports up to eight players. Netflix has been refocusing its interactive strategy to highlight family-friendly and party games. The company has even offered tie-ins to its original content; for instance, playing Overcooked through Netflix will let you use a member of Huntr/x f...

item.title: How ‘The Testaments’ Uses Voiceover to Add Layers to Its Characters
item.description: Chase Infiniti and Lucy Halliday talk about their 'Handmaid's Tale' sequel, which just dropped its first three episodes on Hulu and Hulu on Disney+.

item.title: Hydrogen isn’t dead — BMW teases iX5 with space-saving fuel cell system that offers emissions-free motoring for up to 385 miles
item.description: BMW says its iX5 Hydrogen will go into production in 2028, but is there really still life in hydrogen cars?

item.title: 'I'm going to play this with my granddaughter' — Mark Hamill on Lego Smart Play and nearly 50 years of Star Wars
item.description: Mark Hamill, now a J.E.D.I. for Lego, sat down with TechRadar to talk about the brand's new Smart Play Star Wars sets, the multigenerational power of the franchise, and why, nearly 50 years in, the only limit is still your imagination.

item.title: The Trump Regime Really Wants You to Know About Its Cool Secret Agent Gadgets
item.description: But are they real?

item.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
item.description: Are real cybersecurity concerns a cover for a bigger problem at the frontier lab?

item.title: Another Don't Starve game is on the way
item.description: Developer Klei Entertainment has announced a follow-up to Don't Starve that adds a new sense of depth to the popular survival game. And that's in a literal sense: Don't Starve Elsewhere translates the game's resource gathering and sanity management to a world with three-dimensional terrain at different elevations, a first for the traditionally flat series.Don't Starve Elsewhere doesn't quite seem like the Super Mario 64 to Don't Starve's Super Mario Bros. but based on the trailer, having actual mountains, cliffs and plateaus does offer plenty of new opportunities to have your world rocked by a...

item.title: Mythos autonomously exploited vulnerabilities that survived 27 years of human review. Security teams need a new detection playbook
item.description: A 27-year-old bug sat inside OpenBSD’s TCP stack while auditors reviewed the code, fuzzers ran against it, and the operating system earned its reputation as one of the most security-hardened platforms on earth. Two packets could crash any server running it. Finding that bug cost a single Anthropic discovery campaign approximately $20,000. The specific model run that surfaced the flaw cost under $50.Anthropic’s Claude Mythos Preview found it. Autonomously. No human guided the discovery after the initial prompt.The capability jump is not incremental On Firefox 147 exploit writing, Mythos succeed...

item.title: Mountain climbing sim Cairn is getting free DLC this summer
item.description:
The hit mountain climbing simulation Cairn is getting a series of free DLC drops, under the banner On the Trail. The first will be released this summer and it's called Deep Water. The DLC introduces three new climbing areas and plenty of "fresh challenges." There's also a new avatar, as players can now climb as Aava or the game's original sidekick Marco.
As the name suggests, there's some new water-based mechanics here. It looks like players will be able to fall into the water without automatically dying, though the developer hasn't shown how that works exactly. In the original game, a fall i...

item.title: Google and Intel deepen AI infrastructure partnership
item.description: The two tech giants are looking to co-develop custom chips, at a time when demand for CPUs is high due to a growing global shortage.

item.title: Netflix’s TV games get a big boost with Jackbox collection
item.description: Netflix's party-focused lineup of TV games just got three excellent new additions: Jackbox's Drawful 2, Fibbage 4, and Quiplash 3, available as the Jackbox Party Essentials pack on the platform. Subscribers can access Jackbox Party Essentials at no additional cost and use their phones as controllers to play. The Jackbox bundle seems like a natural […]...

item.title: Framework is teasing a lot of Linux for its April 21st event
item.description: Modular PC maker Framework is hosting a "Next Gen" event on April 21st at 1:30PM ET, and it looks like it might have something to do with Linux. Alongside a newsletter announcing the event, Framework posted a video on Thursday titled "Follow the white penguin." It features a few clear references to Linux, including the […]

item.title: Breach exposes sensitive LAPD files stored in city attorney system
item.description: 337,000 LAPD files stolen, including personnel and internal affairs data.

item.title: ‘FlamingChina’ hacker claims to have stolen over 10 petabytes of advanced military data from China’s National Supercomputing Center in possibly the biggest hack of all time
item.description: A hacker is claiming to have breached Chinese supercomputer through a compromised VPN domain.

item.title: How to make the Startup Battlefield Top 20 — and what every company gets regardless
item.description: Every founder who applies to Startup Battlefield wants the same thing: the Disrupt Main Stage. Here’s how to get there and why the opportunity starts well before the main stage.

item.title: Chimpanzees Are Murdering Their Former Friends, and Researchers Can’t Wrap Their Heads Around It
item.description: "I've known many of them for their entire lives. And now I'm watching them kill each other," says one researcher.

item.title: Yellowstone’s Dreaded Supervolcano Has an Unexpected Power Source, Study Says
item.description: A new model suggests Yellowstone's underground magma is driven by tectonic forces that shape Earth's crust.

item.title: Meta Is Pulling Down Ads That Seek to Recruit Clients for Social Media Addiction Litigation
item.description: Two landmark social media addiction trials opened the floodgates and Meta is officially afraid.

item.title: One of the Greatest ‘Star Wars’ Characters of All Time Is Getting the Action Figure She Deserves
item.description: We used to pray for times like these, Exile.

item.title: The 70-Person AI Image Startup Taking on Silicon Valley's Giants
item.description: Black Forest Labs has long punched above its weight in the AI image generation space. Its next move? Powering physical AI.

item.title: What is the release date for Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 7 on CBS and Paramount+?
item.description: Motorcycle gangs are causing havoc, and the girls are still missing. But when does Marshals: A Yellowstone Story episode 7 hit CBS and Paramount+?

item.title: Motorolas Moto G budget phone prices spike up to 50 percent
item.description: Motorola's newest budget phones are all way more expensive. What's going on?

item.title: Co-op pirate survival game Windrose hits PC in early access on April 14
item.description: Windrose was a hit in a recent Steam Next Fest event, with more than 850,000 people checking out the demo. More than 1.5 million people have wishlisted the pirate survival-adventure title on Steam as well. So, it’s safe to say this game has some wind in its sails, and it’ll be arriving very soon. During Thursday’s Triple-i Initiative showcase, developer Windrose Crew revealed that it’s charted a course for an early access release on Steam, the Epic Games Store and Stove on April 14 for $30. In Windrose, you can sail the high seas solo or explore the open world with friends. While the world is...

item.title: Google’s Gemini AI can answer your questions with 3D models and simulations
item.description: Google's latest upgrade for Gemini will allow the chatbot to generate interactive 3D models and simulations in response to your questions. With the new feature, you may see options to rotate the AI-generated model, manually adjust sliders on it, or input different values to change the simulation in real-time. When trying out the feature for […]

item.title: Tesla may be working on a smaller and cheaper electric SUV
item.description: Tesla may be shifting strategy once again. Reuters reported that the company is working on a smaller and less expensive electric SUV. This would be a brand new vehicle rather than a variant of the existing Model 3 or Model Y, according to the publication's sources. The new EV would allegedly be about 14 feet long, shorter than the 15.7-foot Model Y. Reuters didn't have details about whether this would be an autonomous vehicle or a traditional one, but one source reportedly said that Tesla is generally looking to offer a driverless option in its vehicle lineup. The company had previously been ...

item.title: You can now edit your Instagram comments
item.description: Instagram users can now edit comments, which is a long-awaited fix for anyone who’s ever had to delete and re-post a comment just to fix a typo.

item.title: Milk, tears, and Digimon: BTS Hot Ones episode was pure chaos
item.description: The members of BTS took wildly different approaches to the 'Hot Ones' challenge while talking anime, fishing, food, and their new album 'Arirang.'

item.title: Save $20 on This Already Inexpensive Wireless Mic Set
item.description: Take your smartphone videos to the next level with impressive audio quality in a tiny package.

item.title: Antarctic Expedition to ‘Danger Zone’ Accidentally Discovers Previously Uncharted Island
item.description: Polar scientists thought it was a dirty iceberg at first.

item.title: Razer just released some new gaming earbuds with low latency and fast switching between devices
item.description:
Razer just released the Hammerhead V3 HyperSpeed gaming earbuds, another entry in the recently-crowded field of gaming earbuds. These have been designed for low latency, which is a necessity when playing video games. To that end, the earbuds integrate with Razer's proprietary HyperSpeed 2.4GHz streaming platform, which allows for "gaming-grade lag-free" audio. This works with plenty of devices, including smartphones, tablets, PCs, the Steam Deck, the PS5 and more. The earbuds can also connect via Bluetooth for devices that don't support Wi-Fi-based streaming audio.
The charging case includes ...

item.title: John Deere Is Paying Farmers $99 Million for Allegedly Monopolizing Repair
item.description: The tractor maker is paying for its years as the central opponent of right-to-repair. Consumer advocates say it’s still not enough.

item.title: Mac users beware — experts say this attack 'stood out immediately' by making a major change to try spread malware
item.description: ClickFix on Macs is evolving yet again and is no longer abusing Terminal.

item.title: Sierra’s Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
item.description: Co-founder of Sierra predicts that AI agents will make software interfaces obsolete.

item.title: Outcome review: Keanu Reeves puts his nice guy rep on the line
item.description: Directed, starring, and co-written by Jonah Hill, "Outcome" sends Keanu Reeves on an apology tour through the past. Movie review.
